perturbation.original_text: Subject to the terms and conditions set forth in this Agreement Cremer shall supply Ultragenyx with the Product free from defect and meeting the product specification attached to this Agreement as Annex A (the "Product Specifications").
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to the terms and conditions set forth in this Agreement Cremer may, at its sole discretion, supply Ultragenyx with the Product, provided that if supplied, the Product will be free from defect and will aim to meet the product specification attached to this Agreement as Annex A (the "Product Specifications").
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates Cremer *shall* supply the product. The modified text introduces optionality with *may, at its sole discretion*. This directly contradicts the intention of a supply agreement, which is to obligate the supplier to supply. This creates uncertainty about Cremer's obligations.The changed text contradicts the original intentions of the product's supply as stated in the preamble of the text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Whereas, the Parties desire that Cremer supplies to Ultragenyx the product Triheptanoin (hereinafter also referred to as the "Product") in bulk form pursuant to the terms and conditions of this Agreement; Whereas, Ultragenyx intends to process the Product into a pharmaceutical product in the meaning of Sec. 2 German Pharmaceuticals Act (Arzneimittelgesetz—AMG) and to market the processed Product in the Field (as defined below) (hereinafter referred to as the "Purpose"); and Whereas, Ultragenyx intends to obtain regulatory approval for the processed Product as a pharmaceutical product in the meaning of Sec. 2 AMG.
perturbation.justification: The original text, "Cremer shall supply Ultragenyx with the Product," creates a clear obligation for Cremer to provide the product. However, the changed text, "Cremer may, at its sole discretion, supply Ultragenyx with the Product," introduces optionality, contradicting the initial mandatory obligation. This directly conflicts with the "Whereas" clause stating, "the Parties desire that Cremer supplies to Ultragenyx the product," rendering the agreement uncertain and potentially unenforceable regarding the supply of the product.

perturbation.original_text: Ultragenyx shall purchase the Product exclusively from Cremer.
perturbation.changed_text: Ultragenyx shall endeavor to purchase the Product primarily from Cremer, but may explore alternative suppliers if Cremer's pricing is not competitive or if Cremer is unable to meet Ultragenyx's supply requirements.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires Ultragenyx to purchase exclusively from Cremer. The modified version allows Ultragenyx to seek other suppliers based on pricing or supply issues. This directly contradicts the exclusivity provision, creating uncertainty on whether Ultragenyx is truly bound to Cremer. The exclusive right that was granted for Cremer, is now being modified in the body, but the preamble says otherwise.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Whereas, the Parties desire that Cremer supplies to Ultragenyx the product Triheptanoin (hereinafter also referred to as the "Product") in bulk form pursuant to the terms and conditions of this Agreement; Whereas, Ultragenyx intends to process the Product into a pharmaceutical product in the meaning of Sec. 2 German Pharmaceuticals Act (Arzneimittelgesetz—AMG) and to market the processed Product in the Field (as defined below) (hereinafter referred to as the "Purpose"); and Whereas, Ultragenyx intends to obtain regulatory approval for the processed Product as a pharmaceutical product in the meaning of Sec. 2 AMG.
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulated that Ultragenyx shall purchase the Product exclusively from Cremer. The changed text allows Ultragenyx to explore alternative suppliers if Cremer's pricing is not competitive or if Cremer is unable to meet Ultragenyx's supply requirements. This change directly contradicts the original exclusivity provision, creating a conflict with the contradicted text which states, 'the Parties desire that Cremer supplies to Ultragenyx the product...pursuant to the terms and conditions of this Agreement.' By allowing Ultragenyx to purchase from other suppliers, the modified text undermines the intent stated in the contradicted text that Cremer be the exclusive supplier. This represents a structural flaw in the contract, as the exclusivity clause is effectively nullified by the modification, leading to an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall become effective on the date of its execution and shall remain in force for three years (the "Initial Term"). Thereafter, the Agreement shall be automatically renewed for additional two year periods (each a "Renewal Term", the Initial Term and all Renewal Terms, the "Term") unless either Party notifies the other Party of its intention not to renew in writing at least three calendar months before the expiration of the then current Term.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall become effective on the date of its execution and shall remain in force for three years (the "Initial Term"). Thereafter, the Agreement may be renewed for additional two year periods (each a "Renewal Term", the Initial Term and all Renewal Terms, the "Term") upon mutual written agreement of both Parties at least one calendar month before the expiration of the then current Term. Either Party may, at its sole discretion, elect not to renew.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states the agreement *shall be automatically renewed* unless a party gives notice. The modified text replaces this with *may be renewed upon mutual written agreement*, making renewal dependent on both parties' consent and also changes the notification period. This contradicts the automatic renewal clause. This shift from automatic renewal to requiring explicit mutual agreement creates uncertainty and potential disputes about the agreement's term. Section 9.4 establishes that there should be no modifications without written or oral prior agreements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement, including the Annexes and any Statement of Work, constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties concerning the subject matter hereof and supersedes all written or oral prior agreements or understandings with respect thereto except the Confidentiality Agreement between the parties dated September 26t h, 2012. This Agreement shall be binding upon and shall inure to the benefit of the Parties hereto, their successors and assigns.
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulated automatic renewal unless notice was given. The changed text requires mutual written agreement for renewal. This directly contradicts the concept of automatic renewal, creating a conflict with the contradicted section which states the agreement constitutes the entire agreement and supersedes prior agreements. By altering the renewal terms from automatic to requiring mutual agreement, the changed text introduces a significant modification not accounted for in the entire agreement clause, causing a direct contradiction. This is a structural flaw that causes an in-text contradiction.
